data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Google employs a highly modular microservices architecture, enabling independent development and deployment.
data.technical_spec: Each service is a small, independently deployable unit. Uses Kubernetes for orchestration.

data.title: Global Load Balancing
data.description: Google uses advanced load balancing to ensure high availability and performance.
data.technical_spec: Uses BGP-based routing to distribute traffic across multiple data centers.

data.title: High Availability Infrastructure
data.description: Infrastructure designed for minimal downtime and high fault tolerance.
data.technical_spec: Replication across geographically distributed data centers and automatic failover systems.

data.title: Data Center Optimization
data.description: State-of-the-art data centers optimized for energy efficiency and speed.
data.technical_spec: Custom-built servers and networking equipment with efficient cooling systems.

data.title: Scalable Storage Solutions
data.description: Google's storage solutions can handle vast amounts of data with ease.
data.technical_spec: Use of Google File System (GFS) and Colossus for distributed storage management.
